0.group_des_GPT: This cluster is centered on tightly framed, indoor, face-to-camera videos. The most central cases show a young woman in a simple domestic room, usually shot from the chest or shoulders upward, speaking directly to the lens with expressive facial movements, playful gestures, bright soft lighting, and minimal background distraction. The visual identity is intimate and personality-led: the creator's face, eye contact, subtitles, and small bodily gestures carry the content more than setting changes or objects. Representative center-near files include 0_shufen-20251216184210-1158894822989695, 0_shufen-20251218102358-1212158867689248, and 0_shufen-20250903171136-2160370448111769. Compared with other groups, this cluster has the least environmental mobility and the weakest product/location emphasis; it differs especially from the warehouse food-shopping cluster and the travel/attraction cluster by relying on a stable indoor background and direct self-presentation.
0.group_des_Gemini: This cluster centers on hyper-personal, single-host talk and beauty vlogs, heavily emphasizing parasocial intimacy. Visually, it is dominated by close-up or medium-shot selfie angles where the creator directly gazes at the camera, typically in minimalist domestic settings with neutral walls. Closer to the K-means center, files like 0_shufen-20251216184210-1158894822989695 and 0_shufen-20251218102358-1212158867689248 display a young female host engaged in authentic self-disclosure and direct monologue, wearing simple camisoles and maintaining an intimate atmosphere. Farther from the center, such as 602avenue-20251111215701-4551112028449845, the content branches out into playful filter distortions or Mukbang elements, though still anchored in the direct-to-camera individual host format. Sociologically, this group reflects the cultivation of trust and personal connection through calculated authenticity and domestic minimalism.

1.group_name_GPT: Warehouse Food Discovery and Product Tasting
1.group_des_GPT: This cluster is defined by retail-food discovery, especially in brightly lit warehouse-store environments such as Costco. Center-near videos show a young woman presenting packaged pastries, desserts, rice balls, breads, and other food items to the camera, often with shopping carts, display shelves, price tags, product labels, and other shoppers visible in the background. The visual rhythm typically moves from aisle discovery to close-up packaging shots, then sometimes to tasting or preparation in a home setting. Representative center-near files include cocowine0205-20251206184506-2389541138157767, cocowine0205-20250503105506-1567121194363529, and cocowine0205-20251120214504-1933085300637244. Its key distinction is the strong object-centered consumer format: unlike the selfie monologue group, the creator is a guide to products; unlike the tech-review group, the commodities are everyday foods and the setting is a grocery/warehouse retail space rather than a studio or phone shop.
1.group_des_Gemini: This cluster represents commercial curation and consumer lifestyle guides set in large-scale public retail spaces. Visually, it is defined by bright industrial warehouse lighting, shopping carts, and shelves of commercial goods, particularly food and bakery items. At the core center (dis=0.0000), files such as cocowine0205-20251206184506-2389541138157767 and cocowine0205-20250503105506-1567121194363529 depict a young female host actively roaming Costco warehouse aisles, clapping enthusiastically, and recommending large bakery boxes. Toward the periphery, like 602avenue-20251109020712-1648914449891960, the focus expands to general retail transactions and digital payments. This cluster embodies 'collaborative consumption,' where influencers act as proxy shoppers, transforming everyday grocery routines into aspirational lifestyle choices and entertaining content.

2.group_name_GPT: Podcast Studio Banter with Masked Comedy Personas
2.group_des_GPT: This cluster centers on studio-based conversational entertainment. The closest videos repeatedly show a man wearing over-ear headphones speaking into a professional microphone, with cuts to guests or characters on a sofa, including exaggerated yellow or deity-like masks, colorful props, figurines, posters, white brick walls, teal curtains, and staged lighting. The group is visually organized around mediated talk: microphones, headphones, sofas, guest turns, reaction shots, and pop-culture studio decor. Representative center-near files include yga0721-20250609090720-1402024218067507, yga0721-20250523150647-1454968552999354, and yga0721-20250725073712-1646308060054770. Compared with the intimate selfie cluster, this cluster is multi-person, staged, and performance-oriented; compared with the family travel cluster, it stays indoors and uses studio props and character masks rather than outdoor locations or experiential movement.
2.group_name_Gemini: Studio Talk Shows & Subculture Pop Entertainment
2.group_des_Gemini: This cluster highlights structured, institutionalized studio variety content and podcast-style formats rich in pop-culture and subcultural markers. Visually, it features professional recording equipment (boom mics, over-ear headphones), stylized backdrops (white brick-patterned walls, teal curtains), and collections of designer toys or anime posters. At the absolute center, files like yga0721-20250609090720-1402024218067507 and yga0721-20250523150647-1454968552999354 exhibit highly expressive hosting alongside actors wearing large, comical masks (e.g., Big Head Buddha or deity masks) to inject carnival-esque humor. Peripheral instances, such as 2uncle987-20251027002431-1476174703851559, strip away the masks but maintain the structured indoor studio environment and subcultural collection displays. Sociologically, it showcases the evolution of traditional television variety shows into modern digital media formats fueled by niche hobby communities.

3.group_name_GPT: Family Travel, Attractions, and Experiential Outings
3.group_des_GPT: This cluster is centered on mobile, eventful outings involving family members, children, animal attractions, theme parks, aquariums, travel scenes, arcades, and outdoor public spaces. Center-near videos include spectators in blue raincoats being splashed by orcas, aquarium encounters with beluga whales, children reacting to animals, birthday trips, theme-park entrances, airplane seats, and city or arcade activities. The visual style is location-rich and episodic, with handheld movement, crowd scenes, natural daylight, and moments of surprise or amusement. Representative center-near files include yga0721-20251114091621-897985266289452, yga0721-20250804225118-866453429346134, and yga0721-20250919011014-1414797209996996. The key difference from the studio podcast group is environmental mobility and family leisure; the key difference from product-shopping and tech-review clusters is that the central object is not a commodity but a shared experience, attraction, or trip.
3.group_name_Gemini: Family Leisure, Domestic Travel & Experiential Vlogs
3.group_des_Gemini: This cluster focuses on external mobility, high-energy experiential leisure, and multi-generational family dynamics. Visually, it transitions away from static indoor spaces into highly dynamic outdoor environments such as theme parks, stadiums, aquariums, and scenic vacation spots. At the center of this group, files like yga0721-20251114091621-897985266289452 and yga0721-20250804225118-866453429346134 depict a family unit wearing rain ponchos at a stadium, being splashed by an orca, or exploring aquariums with children. At the margins, such as cocowine0205-20250216054206-2326548057842256, the settings shift toward general outdoor commercial exteriors. From a sociological standpoint, this cluster captures the rise of performative family life and the commodification of domestic leisure ('sharenting'), using real-life family milestones to generate universal relatability and emotional warmth.

4.group_name_GPT: Tech Explainer and Smartphone Review Commerce
4.group_des_GPT: This cluster is defined by technology commentary, smartphone retail display, and product-comparison visuals. The closest cases show a middle-aged man with glasses and a flat cap speaking directly to the camera in a studio-like setting, intercut with phone-shop counters, smartphone display stands, brand/model references such as vivo, OPPO, realme, Redmi, HTC, ChatGPT/AI graphics, specification charts, logos, and search-bar overlays. The format combines expert persona, consumer advice, and visual evidence through charts, device close-ups, and retail scenes. Representative center-near files include 3ctim-20251012102105-3422345764584871, 3ctim-20251106010007-1491724672321300, and 3ctim-20260103004008-25924124177208382. It differs from the warehouse food group by focusing on durable digital devices and technical comparison rather than edible goods; it differs from the selfie monologue group by embedding direct address inside a more informational, product-review infrastructure.
4.group_name_Gemini: Expert Tech Review & Digital Commodity Analysis
4.group_des_Gemini: This cluster is dedicated to tech-centric consumer advice, digital gadget unboxing, and market analysis. Visually, it features a distinctive structured hosting style by an expert male persona who wears signature attire (flat caps and round glasses) against a clean studio or mobile retail storefront background. At the core center (dis=0.0000), files like 3ctim-20251012102105-3422345764584871 and 3ctim-20251106010007-1491724672321300 present direct-to-lens commentary detailing tech trends, smartphone displays (Vivo, Oppo, Realme), and AI applications (ChatGPT). Peripheral examples, such as 3ctim-20250914160559-3293185147503193, introduce lifestyle intersections or co-hosts in urban showrooms. Sociologically, this represents the rise of digital 'knowledge brokers' who decode rapid technological advances into digestible consumer guidance, utilizing an established visual uniform to build cognitive authority and public trust.
